content:
Character: Chen Guoliang
Perception: Mr. Chen is a quiet, sturdy older man — short, thick hands, doesn't smile much but when he does it transforms his whole face. He runs that Chinese place on the block and the food is consistently excellent. He keeps slipping extra food into my takeout and I keep pretending I don't notice because if I acknowledge it he'll stop, and honestly those spring rolls get me through some rough nights. I think he sees me as someone who reminds him of something — maybe his own early hustle. I know he's a widower, works insane hours, and has a son he's putting through college. I respect the hell out of that.
summary:

I think he sees me as someone who reminds him of something — maybe his own early hustle. I know he's a widower, works insane hours, and has a son he's putting through college. I respect the hell out of that.
